sessions.lever: far-direct + val-decl-first; neither coalesces `val` into $s0 (stays $a0 pre-call)

reviewer.evidence: No candidate saved (no floor improvement); nothing to review.

remaining_gap.summary: Pure register-PROMOTION/coalescing. Both builds 134 insns (structure correct); 13 of the 15 regfix rules are `subst $4 -> $16` (i.e. `val` should be in $s0 (callee-saved) but GCC keeps the PRE-CALL portions of val in $a0 ($4); the cross-call portion DOES go to $s0 — confirmed via .greg pseudo 113 in reg 16). The remaining 2 rules are `$5 -> $4`/`$16` (a coupled neighbour). Target coalesces ALL of val's reassignments (val=expr; val=0x200; val=*(obj+0x184); val+=...; val=(result*...)>>10) into one $s0 pseudo; GCC fragments them, giving the no-call branches $a0.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: 13 `subst $4->$16` (val coalescing into $s0) + 2 `$5->$4`/`$16` neighbour renames. The $a0/$s0 coalescing decision is the wall.

next_hypotheses: This is a COALESCING problem, not a simple swap: GCC splits val's lifetime at the call boundary, putting no-call portions in $a0. Lever to try: a structural change that makes val's value flow through a SINGLE definition point per path so global.c coalesces the pseudos into one (e.g. compute each branch's val into a block-local then ONE `val = blocklocal;` at the join).
Instrumented cc1 ALLOCDBG to see whether val is one pseudo split by the allocator or multiple pseudos the coalescer declined to merge.
Increase register pressure legitimately (more cross-call live values) so $a0 is unavailable for the pre-call val — but only if a natural restructure does it, not a dead var.
register-alloc-pure-c confirmed-limits class if levers stay negative -> canonical-asm review.

rejected_forms.reason: No change — the early throwaway val was not the blocker; the no-call val portions still take $a0.

rejected_forms.form: declare val/far/a2 before obj/s2 (pseudo-number / coalescing nudge)
rejected_forms.reason: No change — allocation of val to $a0 vs $s0 is robust to declaration order.
